Tests if empty and non-empty strings are of type 'string'.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the type of two string literals. It uses the `assert` function to verify that both an empty string `""` and a non-empty string `"str"` are of the type `string`. This test ensures that the JavaScript environment correctly identifies string literals as the `string` type. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on type checking for string literals.
